text: Code reviews are a common practice where team members check each other's code before adding it to the main project. This process is important because it helps catch bugs early. When bugs are found soon after being written, they are easier and cheaper to fix than if they are discovered later. Additionally, code reviews encourage knowledge sharing. Developers can learn new techniques and best practices from reviewing others' work. Over time, this improves the overall quality of the team, as everyone becomes more familiar with different parts of the project and writes cleaner, more reliable code.
